short_post: Avoiding peanuts in young children doesn't prevent allergies - it may actually increase risk. The 2015 LEAP trial changed medical advice worldwide, leading health groups to update their guidelines. {{ARTICLE_URL}} #BasedOnScience
long_post: Many parents think avoiding peanuts protects babies from allergies. But groundbreaking research flipped this thinking completely. The 2015 LEAP trial was so powerful that health organizations worldwide had to rewrite their guidelines. According to a NASEM report, recommendations from before this study were excluded from current analysis because they conflicted with the stronger evidence. The study showed that early peanut introduction may actually help prevent allergies - the opposite of what many believed. This major shift in medical advice shows how science evolves when better evidence emerges. Parents should talk to their pediatrician about the best approach for their child's specific situation. {{ARTICLE_URL}}
